hypothesis: The prepared Q0.24 top-k score comparator cutout remains the smallest useful next gate because it directly exercises the full-vocab streamed top-k scoring rule without DDR3 integration, synthesis, or board access. If this invocation can recover Verilator or Python execution, the comparator can be promoted from prepared RTL to simulator-validated evidence; if command execution remains blocked, the correct outcome is to preserve the prepared RTL and record an explicit no-promotion handoff.

commands.cmd: sed reads of h2-int8-l2-selftest-board-comparison.json, h2-v4k-scale-up-summary.json, and h2-vocab-memory-surface-score.json

commands.failure: bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted

bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted
bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted
bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted

commands.cmd: apply_patch add/update of invocation 24 blocked artifacts, run handoff, and AUTONIGHT_STATUS.md

metrics.hardware_block_reason: no board access in this run

decision.rationale: The prepared comparator RTL and testbench still align with the Q0.24 expected-state surface by static readback, but Verilator, Python, git, and listing probes all fail before tool startup with the sandbox loopback error. This iteration therefore cannot produce a simulator PASS, Python replay, synthesis result, or board-facing promotion.
decision.next_gate: Recover command execution, then run: verilator --binary --timing -Wall rtl/task6/task6_q024_topk_score_compare.sv rtl/task6/task6_q024_topk_score_compare_tb.sv && ./obj_dir/Vtask6_q024_topk_score_compare_tb. If it passes, emit h2-q024-topk-comparator-cutout-result.json. If it fails, fix only the tiny comparator/testbench before moving to full-vocab rowwise top-k replay or attention scorecards.
